{{outdated}}

'''Items''' are loose objects dropped by most breakable [[:Category:Building|structures]], [[Enemies|enemies]], and [[Trader|traders]]. Items can be carried around and thrown. Most can be used in the inventory, health panel, or swung.

There are currently '''432''' items (including inaccessible items) in the Itch.io build. <!-- need someone to sort through which ones -->

== Item weight ==

{{Main|Encumbrance}}

[[File:Dressing weight tooltip.png|thumb|The bottom of the tooltip description of [[dressing]]. Notice the weight tag at the top of the block.]]

An item's weight determines the cost it imposes on the player's carrying capacity and the [[Items#Containers|containers]] it can fit in. Weight incentivizes players to make decisions and plan ahead with which items to keep as they encounter more.

Heavier items give more recoil when thrown. Many items lose weight as they lose condition, a trait named '''scale weight''', which linearly goes from full weight at {{SCAV/Condition|100%|label=yes}} to 0.1u at {{SCAV/Condition|0%|label=yes}}.<ref>Item.cs:Item.totalWeight</ref>

== Condition ==

[[File:Dressing condition tooltip.png|thumb|The top of the tooltip description of [[dressing]]. Notice the condition, a colored percentage.]]

An item's condition determines the amount of time or uses it has, in the context of decay rate and condition loss on use respectively. Most items are destroyed when their condition reaches 0%. Many items can have their condition transferred onto other identical items, done by dragging the item onto another item.

The tooltip's condition changes color as it decreases, from {{SCAV/Condition|100%}} to {{SCAV/Condition|50%}}, {{SCAV/Condition|1%}}, and {{SCAV/Condition|0%}}.

Items sometimes give their full effect when used from the inventory, regardless of their condition. This means that, for example, a [[Neural Booster]] at {{SCAV/Condition|1%|label=yes}} and another [[Neural Booster]] at {{SCAV/Condition|15%|label=yes}} will give the same effects when consumed.

* Note that, in general, liquids do scale most of their effects correctly to the actual volume consumed.

{{Anchor|Wetness}}Certain items can become wet upon contact with [[liquids]]. Wet items passively lose condition 6 times faster<ref>Item.cs:Item.HandleDecay()</ref>.
items like the [[shotgun]], [[pistol]], [[makeshift rifle]],  [[rifle]], and [[jetpack]] have special interactions with [[liquids]], as upon getting wet they have a flat +4.5% increased chance of malfunctioning.<ref>GunScript.cs:GunScript.JamChance()</ref> [[Torch|Torches]] and [[Campfire|Campfires]] are immediately set to {{SCAV/Condition|0%|label=yes}} and extinguished upon becoming wet.<ref>CustomItemBehaviour.cs:CustomItemBehaviour.Update()</ref> Items stay wet for 120 seconds.<ref>Item.cs:Item.isWet</ref>

== Crafting ==

{{Main|Crafting}}

Certain items can be combined in the inventory to create new items.

The player can open the crafting menu by pressing C or clicking the button in the bottom left corner of the screen.

Some items have special qualities that make them useful for certain crafting recipes, such as "Hammering" or "Foliage."

== Items by category ==

=== [[File:Trader medical.png|middle|link=]] Medical ===
'''Medical items''' recover [[Limb health|limb health]], treat most physical [[Injuries|injuries]], and delay death. Proper usage of them causes minimal consequence.

Dropped by [[medical crate|medical crates]], [[Container crate|container crates]], [[drop capsule|drop capsules]], and [[Life-pod chest|life pod chests]].

<tabber>
|-| All items = {{Medical items}}
|-| Summary =
</tabber>

=== [[File:Trader drugs.png|link=]] Drugs ===

'''Drugs''' are powerful items that influence the body as a whole. They affect [[Pain|pain]] recovery and change values such as blood viscosity, [[radiation]], [[sickness]], and [[mood]].

Dropped by [[medical crate|medical crates]], [[drop capsule|drop capsules]], and [[Life-pod chest|life pod chests]].

<tabber>
|-| All items = {{Drugs}}
|-| Summary = {{Drug_items_summary}}
</tabber>

=== [[File:Trader food.png|link=]] Food ===

'''Food items''' are consumables that mainly fill the [[hunger]] bar and influence permanent [[mood]].

Dropped by [[Food box]], [[drop capsule|drop capsules]], and [[Life-pod chest|life pod chests]].

<tabber>
|-| All items = {{Food items}}
|-| Summary = {{Food items summary}}
</tabber>

=== [[File:Trader water.png|link=]] Water ===

Water items are consumables that mainly fill the [[thirst]] bar and directly increase permanent [[mood]]. Some give [[energized]] or [[Energized|caffeine]].

Dropped by [[medical crate|medical crates]], [[Food box]], [[drop capsule|drop capsules]], and [[Life-pod chest|life pod chests]]. 

<tabber>
|-| All items = {{Water items}}
|-| Summary = {{Water items summary}}
</tabber>

=== [[File:Trader containers.png|link=]] Containers ===

'''Containers''' store any amount of items, limited only by their capacities and maximum item weights. Containers other than the [[Grav-Bag]] decay while in use (while holding at least one item).

Dropped by [[container crate|container crates]], [[drop capsule|drop capsules]], and [[Life-pod chest|life pod chests]].

<tabber>
|-| All items = {{Container items}}
|-| Summary = {{Container_items_summary}}
</tabber>

=== [[File:Trader tools.png|link=]] Tools ===

<tabber>
|-| All items = {{Tool items}}
|-| Summary = {{Tool_items_summary}}
</tabber>

=== [[File:Trader utility.png|link=]] Utility ===
* Some armor can still deal protection even if they aren't equipped/dropped but they still do take damage like normal. (bug)

<tabber>
|-| All items = {{Utility items}}
|-| Summary = {{Utility items summary}}
</tabber>

=== [[File:Category plant.png|link=]] Plant drops ===

<tabber>

|-| All items = {{Plant drops}}
|-| Summary = {{Plant drops summary}}

</tabber>
=== [[File:Category materials.png|link=]] Materials ===
'''Materials''' are not very useful on their own, however can be crafted into more usable items.
<tabber>
|-| All items = {{Materials}}
|-| Summary = {{Materials summary}}
</tabber>

=== [[File:Category other.png|link=]] Other ===

<tabber>
|-| All items = {{Other items}}
|-| Summary = {{Other items summary}}
</tabber>

[[zh:物品]]
